# Zo Drive

> Zo Drive is a private, owner-operated file manager. It provides a browser
> workspace, a TypeScript SDK, and the `zo-drive` command-line client for the
> same authenticated file store.

This is the agent entry point for a hosted Zo Drive. Read this file before
using the service. It is intentionally concise and should be kept current
whenever a user-facing workflow, API route, CLI command, or authentication
rule changes.

## Service model

- The hosted application normally lives at `/drive`; this file is therefore
  available at `/drive/llms.txt`.
- All paths below are relative to the Zo Drive service root. A client whose
  base URL is `https://drive.example/drive` calls `GET
  https://drive.example/drive/objects`, for example.
- The product currently has one owner account. It is not a public,
  multi-user collaboration service.
- Browser users authenticate with a signed HttpOnly session cookie. Automation
  and CLI clients authenticate with `Authorization: Bearer zdk_...` device
  keys. Treat every device key as a secret.
- Repeated invalid device-key attempts are rate limited and return `429` with
  `Retry-After`. Do not retry a rejected key repeatedly; create a new key or
  reconfigure the client.
- The health endpoint is public. All private file and account actions require
  authentication unless explicitly listed as public below.

## Safe operating rules

- Prefer read-only operations first: health, list, folders, usage, and object
  download.
- Do not create, overwrite, move, trash, permanently delete, revoke a share,
  revoke a device key, change a passcode, alter a quota, or submit a form
  response without the user's explicit instruction.
- `DELETE /objects/{key}` moves an object to Trash; it is not permanent.
  Emptying Trash and deleting a Trash item are permanent actions.
- Never expose, log, or place a bearer device key in a URL.
- Use a safe relative object key. Do not use absolute paths or path traversal.

## API surface

There is not yet a published OpenAPI document. Use the official TypeScript SDK
or CLI for typed access when possible. The routes below are the current HTTP
surface and use JSON except for object upload/download.

### Public routes

- `GET /health` — service health.
- `GET /shared/{id}` — metadata for a public or passcode-protected shared
  file.
- `GET /shared/{id}/content` — shared file content. Protected shares require
  the `x-zo-drive-share-passcode` request header.
- `GET /public/forms/{id}` — published form definition.
- `POST /public/forms/{id}/responses` — submit a form response. This changes
  data; require explicit user confirmation.

### Authenticated routes

- `GET|POST /folders` — list folders or create one.
- `GET|POST /objects` — list objects or upload raw file content. Uploads use
  `x-zo-drive-file-name` and optional `x-zo-drive-path` headers.
- `GET|PATCH|DELETE /objects/{key}` — download, rename/move/copy, or move an
  object to Trash. A copy uses `PATCH` with `copyTo`; it does not transfer the
  file through the client. Existing copy destinations require explicit
  overwrite approval.
- `GET|PUT|DELETE /stars/{key}` and `GET /stars` — manage starred objects.
- `GET /trash`, `PUT /trash/{id}/restore`, `DELETE /trash/{id}`, and
  `DELETE /trash` — inspect, restore, permanently delete, or empty Trash.
- `GET /usage` and `PUT /usage/quota` — storage usage and quota.
- `POST /native-files` and `PUT /native-files/{key}` — create and save Zo
  native documents, spreadsheets, presentations, forms, and pastes.
- `GET|POST /api-keys` and `DELETE /api-keys/{id}` — list, create, or revoke
  device keys. These are browser-session account controls; do not use a
  device key to manage itself.
- `GET|POST /shares`, `PATCH /shares/{id}/passcode`, and
  `DELETE /shares/{id}` — manage file-share and transfer links.
- `POST /forms` and `GET /forms/{id}/responses` — publish a form and retrieve
  its responses.
- `GET /auth/status`, `POST /auth/register`, `POST /auth/login`,
  `POST /auth/logout`, `PATCH /auth/profile`, `POST /auth/password`, and
  `DELETE /auth/account` — owner account and browser-session management.

## Official clients and guides

- [Browser and CLI guides](./?docs=1): User-facing documentation for the
  hosted application.
- [CLI guide](./?docs=1&mode=cli): Install, configure, and use `zo-drive`.
- [Browser guide](./?docs=1&mode=gui): Browser workspace and account controls.
- [Source repository](https://github.com/sayyidkhan/zo-drive): SDK source,
  endpoint implementations, tests, and deployment instructions.

## CLI workflows

Use the CLI rather than hand-assembling HTTP requests where it covers the
task:

```text
zo-drive configure
zo-drive ls [path]
zo-drive exists <key>
zo-drive stat <key> --json
zo-drive upload <local-file> --path <folder>
zo-drive download <key> --output <local-file>
zo-drive mv <source-key> <destination-key>
zo-drive cp <source-key> <destination-key> [--force]
zo-drive rm <key>
zo-drive usage
zo-drive health --json
```

`zo-drive upload --dry-run` and `zo-drive download --dry-run` validate an
operation without transferring file data. `zo-drive rm` moves a file to Trash.

## Maintenance

- Keep this file aligned with `apps/api/src/app.ts`, `packages/sdk/src/index.ts`,
  the CLI, and the browser documentation.
- Add an OpenAPI document here when the HTTP API is stabilized, and link it
  from the API surface section above.
- Update this file in the same change as any new public route, auth change,
  destructive behavior change, or renamed workflow.
